design_summary.name: RV32EC_Zmmul CPU
design_summary.description: Machine-mode-only RV32E core with compressed instruction support, Zmmul multiply support, fetch/decode/execute-writeback pipeline, CSR/trap control, load/store interface, and SPI boot assist integration.

modules.name: alu32
modules.description: 32-bit integer ALU for arithmetic, logical, shift, and compare operations.

behavior_contract.reset_behavior: No reset port. Outputs are pure combinational functions of inputs.
behavior_contract.output_timing: result_o, zero_o, and lt_o shall reflect a_i, b_i, and op_i after combinational settle in the same cycle.
behavior_contract.retention_rules: No state retention. Outputs may change immediately with input changes.
behavior_contract.completion_semantics: Operation completes in the same cycle with no valid/ready handshake.
forbidden_assumptions: Must not assume op_i is one-hot.
Must not assume unsigned compare for lt_o; lt_o is signed less-than.
Must not assume any sequential storage or clock dependency.

modules.name: branch_jump_unit
modules.description: Branch/jump condition evaluation and redirect target/link address generation.

behavior_contract.reset_behavior: No reset port. Outputs are pure combinational functions of inputs.
behavior_contract.output_timing: taken_o, target_o, and link_addr_o shall be valid after combinational settle from current inputs.
behavior_contract.completion_semantics: Branch condition evaluation and target computation are same-cycle operations.
forbidden_assumptions: Must not assume branch_op_i only encodes branches; jumps are also encoded here.
Must not assume link_addr_o depends on taken_o.
Must not assume target_o is only meaningful when taken_o=1; target_o is still computed.

modules.name: immediate_generator
modules.description: Immediate extraction and sign extension for RV32E and compressed-expanded instructions.

behavior_contract.reset_behavior: No reset port. Output is pure combinational function of inputs.
behavior_contract.output_timing: imm_o shall reflect instr_i and imm_type_i after combinational settle.
behavior_contract.completion_semantics: Immediate extraction and sign extension complete in the same cycle.
forbidden_assumptions: Must not assume only base RV32I immediate formats are used; compressed immediate types are also required.
Must not assume all immediates are sign-extended; shamt-style formats may be zero-extended by definition.

modules.name: compressed_decoder
modules.description: Compressed instruction expander and legality checker for supported RV32C subset.

behavior_contract.reset_behavior: No reset port. Outputs are pure combinational functions of c_instr_i.
behavior_contract.output_timing: expanded_instr_o, is_compressed_o, illegal_o, and is_ebreak_o shall settle in the same cycle from c_instr_i.
behavior_contract.completion_semantics: Supported compressed instructions are expanded combinationally; unsupported encodings assert illegal_o.
forbidden_assumptions: Must not assume every 16-bit pattern is legal compressed instruction.
Must not assume is_compressed_o and illegal_o are mutually independent; illegal patterns shall drive illegal_o=1 and is_compressed_o=0.
Must not assume C.EBREAK is illegal; it is legal and separately flagged with is_ebreak_o.

modules.name: decode_control_unit
modules.description: Instruction decode and control generation for ALU, branch, LSU, CSR, multiply, and trap classification.

behavior_contract.reset_behavior: No reset port. Outputs are pure combinational functions of instr_i and instr_is_16b_i.
behavior_contract.output_timing: All decode outputs shall settle in the same cycle from instr_i and instr_is_16b_i.
behavior_contract.completion_semantics: Instruction classification, field extraction, immediate generation, legality checking, and writeback intent are all same-cycle decode functions.
forbidden_assumptions: Must not assume x16..x31 references are legal; they must be flagged illegal.
Must not assume divide/remainder M-extension opcodes are supported; they must trap as illegal.
Must not assume compressed instructions arrive in native 16-bit format here; instr_i is normalized and instr_is_16b_i indicates compressed origin.
Must not assume reg_write_o implies legal instruction; illegal_instr_o has priority at architectural handling.

modules.name: regfile_rv32e
modules.description: 16x32 integer register file with x0 hardwired to zero.

behavior_contract.reset_behavior: Active-low async reset. x0 must read as zero during and after reset. Other architectural registers may reset to zero for implementation simplicity, but correctness only requires x0 hardwired zero and no invalid writes.
state_elements: 16 x 32-bit register array with x0 hardwired to zero
behavior_contract.handshake_protocol: No valid/ready handshake. Two combinational read ports and one synchronous write port.
behavior_contract.output_timing: rdata1_o and rdata2_o are combinational from stored register contents and read addresses. Writes commit on rising edge when we_i=1 and waddr_i!=0.
behavior_contract.retention_rules: Written values persist until overwritten or reset. Writes to x0 are ignored.
behavior_contract.completion_semantics: A legal write completes at the active clock edge; subsequent reads reflect stored state after the edge.
forbidden_assumptions: Must not assume writes to x0 are stored.
Must not assume read-during-write bypass unless explicitly implemented; tests sample after write edge.
Must not assume addresses outside 0..15 are ever driven; top-level legality checks prevent x16..x31 usage.

modules.name: mul_unit
modules.description: Multi-cycle RV32 Zmmul multiplier with busy/done handshake.

behavior_contract.reset_behavior: Active-low async reset clears busy_o, done_o, and internal operation state.
behavior_contract.handshake_protocol: start_i is sampled when busy_o=0. A multiply request begins on an accepted start_i pulse. busy_o remains asserted while operation is in progress. done_o asserts for completion indication when result_o becomes valid.
behavior_contract.output_timing: busy_o changes on clock edges. result_o is valid at completion. done_o is a completion pulse/indication aligned to completion edge.
behavior_contract.retention_rules: Captured operands and result are retained across cycles while busy or until next accepted request. done_o must not stick high beyond completion indication behavior.
behavior_contract.completion_semantics: Operation completes after implementation-defined positive latency. Low 32 bits of signed product are returned in result_o.
forbidden_assumptions: Must not assume single-cycle multiply.
Must not assume start_i is accepted while busy_o=1.
Must not assume result_o is meaningful before completion.
Must not assume unsigned multiplication semantics; low 32 bits correspond to signed integer product behavior in tests.

modules.name: load_store_unit
modules.description: Address generation and variable-latency data memory handshake interface.

behavior_contract.reset_behavior: Active-low async reset clears any outstanding request tracking and deasserts busy behavior.
state_elements: latched effective address/control for wait-state hold
latched load data register if needed
behavior_contract.handshake_protocol: When req_i=1, the LSU issues mem_req_o with computed address and we qualifier. If mem_ready_i=0, request remains outstanding and busy_o=1 until completion. For loads, load_data_o becomes valid when completion occurs. For stores, completion is acknowledged by mem_ready_i.
behavior_contract.output_timing: Request outputs may assert in same cycle as req_i. busy_o is level-sensitive to outstanding transaction state. load_data_o is meaningful on/after completing load.
behavior_contract.retention_rules: Outstanding request parameters must be held stable across wait states until mem_ready_i=1.
behavior_contract.completion_semantics: A transaction completes when req_i has been issued and mem_ready_i=1 for that request. busy_o deasserts once no request remains pending.
forbidden_assumptions: Must not assume zero-wait-state memory.
Must not assume req_i is only pulsed for one cycle; implementation must handle held requests coherently.
Must not assume byte enables vary by size in this scope; inferred tests require full-word be=4'hF.

modules.name: csr_trap_controller
modules.description: Machine-mode CSR storage, trap entry, interrupt handling, and exception return support.

behavior_contract.reset_behavior: Active-low async reset initializes machine-mode CSR state to defined reset values, clears mepc, and deasserts trap_taken_o.
state_elements: optional mie/mip subset or equivalent interrupt-enable state
behavior_contract.handshake_protocol: CSR read is direct by csr_re_i/csr_addr_i. CSR write occurs on clock edge when csr_we_i=1. trap_i or qualified irq_ext_i initiates trap entry. mret_i initiates exception return behavior.
behavior_contract.output_timing: csr_rdata_o is combinational or same-cycle readable from current CSR state. trap_taken_o is asserted in response to trap/interrupt entry conditions. trap_vector_o reflects mtvec. mepc_o reflects stored mepc state.
behavior_contract.retention_rules: CSR contents persist until written, trap-updated, interrupt-updated, or reset. mepc and mcause update on trap/interrupt entry.
behavior_contract.completion_semantics: Trap entry atomically saves trap_pc_i to mepc, updates mcause, updates status as required, and redirects to mtvec. MRET restores return state and exposes mepc for fetch redirect.
forbidden_assumptions: Must not assume vectored mtvec mode support.
Must not assume nested interrupt handling or prioritization beyond single external interrupt.
Must not assume irq_ext_i always causes entry; machine interrupt enable state may gate it.
Must not assume unsupported CSR addresses have architecturally meaningful storage.

modules.name: fetch_unit
modules.description: Program counter control, fetch buffering, and redirect handling for 16-bit/32-bit instruction flow.

behavior_contract.reset_behavior: Active-low async reset loads PC from reset_vector_i, clears buffered instruction valid state, and enables fetch_req_o.
behavior_contract.handshake_protocol: Fetch request is continuously driven when front-end is active and not fail-stopped externally. instr_valid_i qualifies capture of instr_rdata_i into internal buffer. stall_i holds PC and buffered state. flush_i invalidates buffered instruction.
behavior_contract.output_timing: pc_o reflects current fetch PC state. Redirects and sequential increments take effect on clocked state update. instr_o/instr_valid_o reflect buffered fetch contents. halfword_sel_o reflects current PC halfword selection.
behavior_contract.retention_rules: On stall_i=1, PC and buffered instruction state are retained. On flush_i=1, buffered valid state is cleared. Redirect priority is trap over mret over branch/jump over sequential advance.
behavior_contract.completion_semantics: A fetch step completes when PC state advances or redirects and any valid instruction response is captured into buffer.
forbidden_assumptions: Must not assume only 32-bit instructions; sequential increment depends on curr_is_16b_i.
Must not assume flush_i and stall_i are mutually exclusive; flush must still invalidate buffered state consistently.
Must not assume branch redirects outrank trap or mret.

modules.name: boot_rom_loader_assist
modules.description: SPI boot assist and fail-stop control for boot image load and validation.

behavior_contract.reset_behavior: Active-low async reset clears busy_o, boot_done_o, boot_fail_o, and fail_stop_o.
behavior_contract.handshake_protocol: start_i initiates boot assist when boot_mode_spi_i=1. Completion is determined by spi_done_i with validation checks, or immediate failure by spi_error_i. fail_stop_o latches terminal failure state.
behavior_contract.output_timing: State outputs update on clock edges. boot_done_o and boot_fail_o indicate terminal result. fail_stop_o remains asserted after failure until reset.
behavior_contract.retention_rules: Once fail_stop_o asserts, it remains high until reset. Success/failure status remains consistent with terminal state.
behavior_contract.completion_semantics: Boot succeeds only if spi_done_i=1 and all validation conditions pass, including header_magic_ok_i, program_size_i<16384, start_addr_ok_i=1, and validation_ok_i=1 when checked.
forbidden_assumptions: Must not assume boot assist runs when boot_mode_spi_i=0.
Must not assume spi_done_i implies success; header/range/validation checks are required.
Must not assume fail_stop_o is recoverable without reset.

modules.name: execute_writeback_unit
modules.description: Execute-stage orchestration for ALU/multiply/branch/CSR/load-store result selection and writeback generation.

behavior_contract.reset_behavior: Active-low async reset clears output-valid state, stall_o, branch_taken_o, and trap signaling.
state_elements: optional held X-stage context for outstanding load/mul/store
writeback output registers if registered implementation is used
behavior_contract.handshake_protocol: Consumes one decoded instruction context at a time. For loads, waits for load_ready_i before writeback. For stores, request completes when memory side is accepted/ready. For multiply, stalls while mul_busy_i=1 and completes when result is available. trap_o suppresses normal commit.
behavior_contract.output_timing: ALU, branch/jump, and CSR results may be produced in the same cycle. stall_o is asserted while operation is incomplete. wb outputs are valid only when wb_we_o=1. branch redirect outputs are meaningful when branch_taken_o=1.
behavior_contract.retention_rules: During stalls, the active instruction context must be retained until completion or trap handling. wb_we_o must remain deasserted until completion of load or multiply. Trap conditions prevent writeback.
behavior_contract.completion_semantics: Exactly one architectural result source is selected per instruction: ALU, load, mul, CSR read, or jump link. Branches/stores generally do not write registers unless jump link applies. Illegal instruction or ebreak raises trap_o with matching cause.
forbidden_assumptions: Must not assume all instructions write back.
Must not assume branch resolution occurs in decode; it occurs here.
Must not assume load_ready_i and mul_busy_i are mutually exclusive globally; only current op type determines stall source.
Must not allow normal writeback in same instruction instance that raises trap_o.

modules.name: rv32ec_zmmul_cpu_top
modules.description: Top-level integration of fetch, decode, execute/writeback, register file, CSR/trap, LSU, multiplier, compressed decode, and boot assist with global stall/flush control.

signals.name: boot_start
signals.description: Start signal for boot ROM loader assist

behavior_contract.reset_behavior: Active-low async reset initializes pipeline state, starts from reset vector, clears trap_taken and fail_stop, and leaves data-side idle.
state_elements: integration wiring state where submodules are sequential
behavior_contract.handshake_protocol: Instruction fetch uses if_req/if_pc_addr with if_instr_valid response. Data memory uses lsu_req/lsu_we/lsu_addr/lsu_wdata/lsu_be with lsu_ready response. Boot assist uses boot_start and completion/failure indications. Interrupts are sampled via irq_ext and routed through CSR/trap control.
behavior_contract.output_timing: Top-level outputs reflect submodule outputs and pipeline control. Fetch and LSU requests may persist across waits. trap_taken indicates trap/interrupt redirect. fail_stop latches terminal boot failure behavior.
behavior_contract.retention_rules: During load or multiply stalls, younger stages must hold state. On branch/jump/trap redirect, younger F/D contents are flushed. In fail-stop, instruction and data requests must be suppressed.
behavior_contract.completion_semantics: One instruction may commit per completed X-stage operation. Redirects and traps have priority over younger instructions. Boot fail-stop is terminal until reset.
forbidden_assumptions: Must not redesign module hierarchy or bypass named submodules.
Must not assume no compressed instructions; top must honor 16-bit and 32-bit instruction flow.
Must not assume memory is always ready.
Must not assume boot mode and execute mode are simultaneously unrestricted; fail-stop suppresses activity.

packages.name: rv32ec_zmmul
packages.description: Global constants, CSR addresses, trap causes, ALU ops, branch ops, immediate types, and pipeline control typedefs

external_interfaces.name: instruction_fetch_interface
external_interfaces.description: Simple instruction memory request/response interface using if_req, if_pc_addr, if_instr_rdata, and if_instr_valid

external_interfaces.name: data_memory_interface
external_interfaces.description: Simple load/store handshake interface using lsu_req, lsu_we, lsu_addr, lsu_wdata, lsu_be, lsu_rdata, and lsu_ready

external_interfaces.name: external_interrupt_interface
external_interfaces.description: Single machine external interrupt input via irq_ext

external_interfaces.name: spi_boot_status_interface
external_interfaces.description: Boot assist status/control integration via boot_mode_spi, boot_start, spi_boot_done, spi_boot_fail, and fail_stop
